summaryText: Pastor Darrison Tellez preached from 1 Samuel 1, exploring the story of Hannah's deep anguish and longing for a child while facing a closed womb. He framed this as a powerful metaphor for the 'waiting seasons' we all experience, where a God-given purpose or desire feels barren and unfulfilled. The message emphasized that God sometimes closes doors not to cancel our purpose, but to prepare us, press out our authentic selves, and refine our character. Pastor Darrison encouraged the congregation to see waiting not as punishment but as a time of divine preparation, where God uses provocation and pressure to elevate us. He highlighted Hannah's persistent, 'foolish' faithfulness in prayer as a model, urging listeners to 'pray on until something happens' (PUSH). The sermon concluded with the hopeful declaration that what God has called, no person or circumstance can ultimately cancel, and that our breakthrough is often closest when we feel we are at our breaking point.
mainPoints: A closed door from God does not mean a canceled purpose; it is often a season of preparation and refinement.
God uses provocation and pressure in our waiting seasons not to scare us, but to search, sift, and elevate us for our destiny.
Faithful persistence in prayer, which may look like foolishness to the world, is the key to pushing through until God's promise is fulfilled.

versesMentioned.text: Whenever the day came for Elkanah to sacrifice, he would give portions of the meat to his wife Peninnah and to all her sons and daughters. But to Hannah he gave a double portion because he loved her, and the LORD had closed her womb... In the course of time Hannah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Samuel, saying, 'Because I asked the LORD for him.'
Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a longing fulfilled is a tree of life.
What, then, shall we say in response to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?
Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
The words 'once more' indicate the removing of what can be shaken—that is, created things—so that what cannot be shaken may remain.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise; God chose the weak things of the world to shame the strong. God chose the lowly things of this world and the despised things—and the things that are not—to nullify the things that are, so that no one may boast before him.

Monday.scripture: 1 Samuel 1:10-11 (NIV) - 'In her deep anguish Hannah prayed to the LORD, weeping bitterly. And she made a vow...'
Monday.action: Identify one area of 'deep anguish' or longing in your life. Be honest with God about it in prayer today, just as Hannah was.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I bring my deepest longing to you. I trust you with my heart's ache and ask for your perspective in this waiting season.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 13:12 (NIV) - 'Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a longing fulfilled is a tree of life.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on how deferred hope has affected you. Write down one promise from God's Word that you can cling to for encouragement.
Tuesday.prayer: God, when my heart feels sick from waiting, remind me that you are the fulfiller of hope. Strengthen my faith in your timing.

Wednesday.scripture: Hebrews 12:27 (NIV) - '...so that what cannot be shaken may remain.'
Wednesday.action: Consider what in your life feels 'shaken' right now. Make a list of the eternal, unshakable truths about God's character that remain firm.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, in the shaking, reveal what is eternal. Burn away what is temporary and solidify in me what is of you.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 1:27 (NIV) - 'But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise...'
Thursday.action: Is there a prayer or act of obedience that makes you feel 'foolish'? Commit to doing it today, trusting God's perspective over others'.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to be 'foolish' for you. Use my faithful persistence to shame the wisdom of the world and bring you glory.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness...'
Friday.action: Re-examine a personal prayer request. Pray for it again, but first ask for God's kingdom purpose to be fulfilled in and through it.
Friday.prayer: Lord, above my desire for an answer, I seek your kingdom. Align my heart with your purposes in this situation.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 8:31 (NIV) - 'If God is for us, who can be against us?'
Saturday.action: Speak this truth over your closed door or waiting season. Declare aloud, 'God is for me in this,' and rest in that assurance.
Saturday.prayer: Thank you, God, that you are for me. I reject any lie that you are against me. I stand at the door in faith, trusting your good plan.
